india_trends.title: Diet coke runs dry in Indian cities as Iran war triggers aluminium can shortage
india_trends.summary: Diet Coke is disappearing from shelves across major Indian cities. A global shortage of aluminium beverage cans, exacerbated by the Iran war, is hitting supplies. india_trends.title: DSP MF sees attractive entry point in large private banks
india_trends.summary: “Without pencilling in any re-rating, large private banks present an attractive opportunity for investors today,” said DSP in a note. “More price decline will make them more attractive.”

india_trends.title: War Pushes Japan Equity Fund Into Cash, Eyes Re-Entry in Banks
india_trends.summary: A top-performing Japan equity fund has cut exposure to cyclical stocks and increased cash levels amid heightened uncertainty surrounding the Iran war, and is now waiting for the moment to get back into bank and industrial shares.

india_trends.summary: "The financial services sector has the biggest weight on benchmark Nifty; so when there is broad-based selling, banking and financial services' share in foreign selling tends to be higher," said U R Bhat, co-founder & director, Alphaniti.

india_trends.summary: Indian markets saw significant gains on Tuesday, with the Nifty and Sensex rising over 0.9% and 1% respectively. india_trends.title: Unravelling Hedge Fund Trade Derails Boom in Taiwan Convertibles
india_trends.summary: A lucrative hedge fund trade in Taiwanese convertible bonds has been derailed by regulatory changes, disrupting issuance in one of the hottest capital markets for the island’s tech sector.

india_trends.title: Vanguard Scoops Up Treasuries as Iran Conflict Lifts Yields
india_trends.summary: Vanguard is boosting its holdings of Treasuries, taking advantage of higher yields following the Middle East conflict to lock in rates and hedge against the risks of a potential growth slowdown.
